event_blocks.summary: Seated at a wooden desk in a warmly lit, stylish room, a Man in Teal Henley Shirt, speaking with an engaging British accent, directly addresses the camera for his YouTube channel, 'Man For Himself,' which focuses on hair product reviews, tutorials, grooming, skincare, and fragrance. He animatedly uses expressive hand gestures—pointing, clasping, making open-palmed motions, pinching, and sweeping—while discussing his Tom Ford fragrance collection, which he states totals '19 in total' across signature and private blends, describing them as 'accessible luxury.' Behind him, a Computer Monitor displays changing product images related to men’s grooming and lifestyle, including Tom Ford fragrances. The Desk Decor is consistently visible, featuring a vase of colorful flowers, a lit candle, several dark fragrance bottles, and a clear glass mug with a dark liquid, contributing to a cozy ambiance alongside Framed Artwork and Green leafy Plants. Robin explains the video is 'therapy' to cope with feeling 'a bit shit' and 'over this lockdown,' mentioning his strategy of wearing a 'full blue outfit' and drinking coffee. Midway through, a 'SUBSCRIBE NOW!' graphic appears, followed by a split-screen showing his social media handle '@MANFORHIMSELF' with links to Instagram, Twitter, and TikTok, alongside a stylized photo. He passionately recalls his first Tom Ford fragrance, 'Tom Ford Tobacco Vanille,' describing it as 'luxurious,' 'deep,' with 'sweetness of the vanilla' and 'great masculine, evening smokiness of the tobacco,' which shifted his scent preferences. He introduces 'Oud Wood' from the 'private blend collection,' presenting a TOM FORD OUD WOOD Perfume Bottle and a white applicator stick, describing it as a 'safe bet' for men who want to 'look and feel good' and 'one of the easiest to wear' from the collection, noting its "warm notes" including "amber, vanilla, tonka bean, and sandalwood," and that it's "masculine, but it's not brutish." He humorously shares an anecdote about an ex but is now "back onto it" and "loves it," mentioning its expansion into grooming products. He then introduces 'Oud Wood Intense' as a livelier, punchier, and spicier version with ginger, compared to a previous "warm hug" scent, showing a close-up of the TOM FORD OUD WOOD INTENSE EAU DE PARFUM 50 ML bottle with on-screen text '2 OUD WOOD INTENSE MAN FOR HIMSELF' and 'ALL LINKED IN DESCRIPTION,' assuring viewers he will "link them all in the description" for checking prices, noting many are already on "manfromself.com."

Computer Monitor.description: Displays a website with changing product images, likely related to men’s grooming or lifestyle, positioned behind the man on the desk.

Desk Decor.description: Includes a vase of colorful flowers, a lit candle, several dark bottles (possibly colognes or skincare products), and a clear glass mug with a dark liquid, arranged neatly on the desk.

Man in Teal Henley Shirt.description: A man with short dark hair and light stubble, wearing a teal long-sleeve henley shirt, seated at a desk, speaking directly to the camera with an engaging, enthusiastic tone and a clear British accent, using expressive hand gestures while holding and demonstrating a perfume bottle, describing scents and providing product information.

Framed Artwork.description: Two framed pieces on the wall behind the man—one abstract and one possibly depicting a bird—adding to the room’s aesthetic appeal.

Social Media Graphic.description: A split-screen overlay appearing mid-segment showing '@MANFORHIMSELF' with links to Instagram, Twitter, and TikTok, alongside a stylized photo of the man posing with arms crossed, confirming his online persona.

The man explicitly names 'Oud Wood' and 'Oud Wood Intense', describing it as 'livelier,' 'punchier,' 'spicier,' and containing 'ginger,' noting it's a 50ml eau de parfum.

On the desk to his left, a vase of colorful flowers, a lit candle, and several dark bottles, explicitly identified as his Tom Ford fragrance collection, are neatly arranged alongside a clear glass mug containing a dark liquid, which he later implies is coffee, part of his coping mechanism. event_blocks.summary: The Man in Teal Henley Shirt, seated at his desk, presented two distinct Tom Ford fragrances. Initially, he discussed a dark, square-shaped TOM FORD OUD MINERALE Perfume Bottle, describing its "salty personality" as "slightly more abrasive" and "sharper" than "Oud Wood," which he characterized as "relaxing, warming, rounded" and "less punchy." He noted the Oud Minerale's "playful" scent with "pink peppercorn" and contrasted it with the "ginger in the intense." He also mentioned a "seaweed" or "aquatic sort of note" in another fragrance, deeming it not an everyday scent but one he needed to be "in the mood for," while calling "Oud Wood" a "safe bet" and "gorgeous on the skin." Later, he focused on the TOM FORD OMBRÉ LEATHER Perfume Bottle, describing it as "matte black" and the fragrance as "absolutely beautiful," featuring notes of "Jasmine Sambac, Black Leather, there's Amber," and "a cooling spice of the cardamom." He distinguished this luxurious, soft calf leather scent from tougher, punkier leathers, suggesting it could be a "signature scent" worn "all year round." Throughout the presentation, the Man in Teal Henley Shirt used a white applicator stick for demonstrations, spoke with clear articulation and expressive hand gestures, and was framed by a Computer Monitor displaying product images, Desk Decor with flowers and a candle, Framed Artwork, and Plants, all under warm, intimate lighting.

event_blocks.summary: The Man in Teal Henley Shirt, seated at a warmly lit desk, begins by holding the TOM FORD BEAU DE JOUR perfume bottle, unscrewing its cap to reveal and demonstrate a white applicator stick, describing the fragrance as having the powderiness of lavender, freshness, mintiness from geranium, and grounding from amber, which he personally favors for its lightness, luxury, and warmth. He emphasizes the fragrance's longevity and mature, grounded profile, before discussing its price point. On-screen text overlays appear, stating 'ALL LINKED IN DESCRIPTION' and '5 BEAU DE JOUR MAN FOR HIMSELF'. He then compares the BEAU DE JOUR with the TOM FORD OUD WOOD perfume bottle, identifying BEAU DE JOUR as the 'signature' version and discussing marketing strategies involving celebrity endorsements. Transitioning, he describes a fragrance with notes of basil, patchouli, lavender, and geranium as fresh, bright, masculine-ish, and wearable, using the white applicator stick for demonstration. A close-up shot shows the TOM FORD OUD MINERALE perfume bottle. He then discusses 'Vert de Fleur,' noting its discontinuation and his dislike for its overly green scent, linking it to fragrance trends in 2020 favoring citrus profiles over green ones. He then picks up the TOM FORD OMBRÉ LEATHER perfume bottle, illustrating application with the white applicator stick, and audibly shifts to discussing floral notes. An on-screen text overlay '6 VERT DE FLEUR MAN FOR HIMSELF' appears, followed by a social media graphic. He then introduces the ROSE PRICK product, describing Tom Ford fragrances as boundary-pushing and creative, noting the 'double entendre' and 'buzzy' nature of scents like 'Fucking Fabulous,' 'Lost Cherry,' and 'Rose Prick,' which garner press coverage. Despite finding Rose Prick initially disappointing, he describes its story from Tom Ford's Rose Garden and calls the scent an easy-to-wear rose, with a close-up shot of the bottle reinforcing its identity. He further elaborates on Rose Prick, contrasting common perceptions of rose fragrances with its fun and wearable profile for men, detailing notes of turmeric, Sichuan pepper, May Rose, Bulgarian Rose, Turkish Rose, patchouli, and tonka bean, while personally not detecting much spiciness. He describes the sweet notes as caramel, comparing it to a boiled sweet with a juicy center, and reiterates his strong liking for the fragrance, calling it a journey, bright, gorgeous, and luxurious, playfully suggesting 'caramel prick' as an alternative name. He then announces a shift to lavender, opening a box to reveal the TOM FORD LAVENDER EXTREME perfume bottle and white applicator stick, declaring it a favorite from a limited edition run. He explains Lavender Extreme features three or five types of lavender, is not typical lavender, and is playful and lively with Italian citrus accents and carrot seed. He further describes the scent as having a creamy woodiness of benzoin, clarifying it's not whimsical or throwaway, and suggests it pairs well with a white shirt and jeans. He briefly compares it with OUD WOOD, OUD MINERALE, BEAU DE JOUR, and ROSE PRICK, and mentions the popular 'F***ing Fabulous' fragrance. The segment concludes with him holding and discussing the TOM FORD OMBRÉ LEATHER perfume bottle.

event_blocks.summary: The Man in Teal Henley Shirt, seated at a desk adorned with Desk Decor including flowers, a lit candle, and Plants, and framed by Framed Artwork and a Computer Monitor displaying product images, reviews several TOM FORD perfume bottles. Initially, he holds a dark, square TOM FORD FUCKING FABULOUS EAU DE PARFUM 50 ML bottle and a white applicator stick, discussing its marketing with a slightly sarcastic tone, stating, "the marketing is perfection," and how they "dialed up that shock factor," advising offended viewers to "check yourself" and "get a grip." He demonstrates the applicator, then compares the FUCKING FABULOUS bottle to TOM FORD OUD WOOD and TOM FORD LAVENDER EXTREME bottles on his desk, initially expressing excitement but later deeming it "fine." An On-Screen Text Overlay with '8 LAVENDER EXTREME MAN FOR HIMSELF' and a Social Media Graphic '@MANFORHIMSELF' appear. He describes the FUCKING FABULOUS scent as "beyond fragrant," focusing on the almond note over sage, lavender, tonka, cashmeran, or woods, before moving out of frame. Later, holding the TOM FORD OUD WOOD bottle and applicator, he humorously advises, "don't judge people on what they wear, but also judge people on what they wear," and describes a persona associated with FUCKING FABULOUS. He then transitions to a clear, ribbed TOM FORD BEAU DE JOUR Perfume Bottle and a clear, square TOM FORD LAVENDER EXTREME Perfume Bottle, concluding he likes it but is "not that impressed," playfully calling it "Damian... Demo in a bottle." Finally, he introduces the clear, ribbed glass TOM FORD GREY VETIVER EAU DE TOILETTE Perfume Bottle with a silver cap, calling it a "classic" and comparing it to Black Orchid. He demonstrates the White Applicator Stick, describing the GREY VETIVER scent as "mass market" and "mainstream" but "really heavy on the citruses" yet "darker, deeper," and "more luxurious," with "spices" and "woodiness," deeming it "playful and it's easy to wear." An On-Screen Text Overlay '10 GREY VETIVER MAN FOR HIMSELF' appears.

event_blocks.summary: Seated at a warmly lit desk, the Man in Teal Henley Shirt engages the camera, initially discussing Tom Ford fragrances. The desk is adorned with Desk Decor, including flowers, a lit candle, and dark bottles, alongside Framed Artwork and Plants, with a Computer Monitor displaying product images in the background. He notes the prominent notes of 'Amberwood, Cedarwood, Sandalwood, Birchwood,' along with 'leather and patchouli,' confirming 'Cedar' as a key scent, and mentions becoming 'nose blind' due to the intensity. event_blocks.summary: The Man in Teal Henley Shirt, seated at a warmly lit desk, begins by discussing the TOM FORD NOIR ANTHRACITE Perfume Bottle and its White Applicator Stick, describing its blend of "woods," "greens," "leather," "ginger," and "pepper," deeming it "more wearable" and rating it "a solid six out of ten" before demonstrating the applicator's use. He then introduces the TOM FORD COSTA AZZURRA Perfume Bottle, calling it "pure summer," "gorgeous," "light and bright," and a "destination in a bottle," distinguishing between its "Eau de Parfum" and "Aqua" versions, focusing on the "sun-drenched" "Eau de Toilette" which he finds "fresher" and "more playful" with "more citrus" and a "sharp" lemon note, making it "livelier" and "easier to wear." He also reviews the TOM FORD MANDARINO DI AMALFI ACQUA Perfume Bottle, describing its "beautiful pillared bottle with the gold of the top" as having a "solar quality," and noting its "summer" and "aqua" characteristics as an Eau de Toilette, with "more orange and mandarin," making it "far juicier" and evoking a Negroni cocktail. He further discusses other fragrances, including "Lost Cherry" as a "big cult hit" with "sweetness" and "cherry pop," and "Vanille Fatale," which he finds "punchy" and smelling "oily" with "Madagascar vanilla," contrasting it with "Tobacco Vanille" which he found "sickly sweet." The man expresses difficulty wearing a specific "vanilla" fragrance that "gets really caught onto the back of my throat," and mentions owning "six signature and 13 private blend" fragrances. He shares personal details, stating he is "32" and will be "33 in July, July the 6th," and encourages viewers to "press subscribe" and share their favorite fragrances. The segment concludes with the announcement of "fragrance pages on manforhimself.com" and more videos there, followed by a sign-off and a circular viewfinder effect showcasing close-ups of objects, oranges, and a blue bottle, before the 'M for H' logo and 'MAN FOR HIMSELF' tagline appear with a jingle. Throughout the review, the Computer Monitor behind him displays changing product images, while Desk Decor including colorful flowers, a lit candle, and dark bottles, along with Framed Artwork and Plants, contribute to a cozy, curated ambiance, all under soft, warm lighting.
